meta_title: Montana Worker Classification State Test 2026 | Teamed
meta_description: Montana worker classification 2026: AB test for UI (MCA § 39-71-417), no Prong B required — worker must be free from direction AND control AND in an independently established business. ICCU issues Independent Contractor Exemption Certificates. Construction Contractor Registration required under § 39-9-201. Misclassification penalties under § 39-3-209.
hero_image_alt: Montana state capitol building in Helena under a clear blue sky, with the historic copper dome visible above the legislative grounds.
key_statutes: MCA § 39-71-417 (AB test — direction and control + independently established business)
test_type: AB (Prongs A and C only — Prong B not required)
notes: Montana is one of a distinct group of states that runs only Prongs A and C of the ABC framework for UI and workers' comp purposes — the 'AB test'. The absence of Prong B (outside usual course of business) is the defining structural difference from strict ABC-test states. The ICCU certificate regime is Montana's distinctive administrative compliance layer. Tagline-close bylined to Tom Price-Daniel consistent with sprint pattern.
